nodes.label: AI draft as structural roadmap
nodes.grounding_span: It prepopulates the draft for me, which is helpful to see the broader topics, number of speakers, and how much each speaker talks

nodes.label: AI accuracy in speaker attribution
nodes.grounding_span: It can be pretty good at understanding speaker's voices and attributes them correctly, mostly. But sometimes, there are two speakers and it decides that one speaker actually sounds like 3-4 different speakers

nodes.label: AI handling of speech variability
nodes.label_negative: AI failure with atypical speech patterns
nodes.grounding_span: Speakers who stutter a lot or break up their sentences can trip up the model and it's preferable to type from scratch for those tougher file types

nodes.label: AI as cost-effective solution for customers
nodes.label_negative: AI as threat to human livelihood
nodes.grounding_span: The AI solutions are often cheaper for customers to use, so we have had customers flocking to try them, which loses work for us

nodes.label: AI data confidentiality and security
nodes.label_negative: AI data exposure and lack of privacy
nodes.grounding_span: I think it's an area that should have a clear clause around security and confidentiality. Some are court cases that come through and require that; but if it trains an AI model, is it really going to stay confidential, always?

nodes.label: Work efficiency and accuracy
nodes.grounding_span: It prepopulates the draft for me, which is helpful

nodes.label: Professional competence and quality
nodes.grounding_span: some customers also come back to the human side after experiencing the AI's 'work' which can be very substandard

nodes.label: Job security and livelihood
nodes.grounding_span: some people have lost their livelihood because the work just isn't there now

nodes.label: Data privacy and ethical use
nodes.grounding_span: it should be held privately, and have clear rules around its collection

nodes.label: Positive appreciation for AI draft as starting point
nodes.grounding_span: I think having it prepopulate a transcript is the best way forward

nodes.label: Frustration with AI hallucination and misattribution
nodes.grounding_span: The model can hallucinate entire sentences of script that don't exist

nodes.label: Concern about AI replacing human jobs
nodes.grounding_span: some people have lost their livelihood because the work just isn't there now

nodes.label: Ambivalence about AI data confidentiality
nodes.grounding_span: It's perplexing to think about, because the data is just there, it doesn't go anywhere. But it should be held privately

nodes.label: Comparative evaluation — weighs AI draft against own listening
nodes.grounding_span: Once I start listening to the audio to proofread, it's clear where the model does well and where it slips

nodes.label: Pragmatic adaptation — adjusts workflow based on file difficulty
nodes.grounding_span: it's preferable to type from scratch for those tougher file types
